summary: Front‑of‑board audience tape captures a powerhouse second set highlighted by Scarlet > Fire, Space/So What and a soaring Fire on the Mountain.
review: The recording is an excellent front‑of‑board audience capture that places the listener right in the arena with clear balance and minimal hiss. Musically the show shines especially in the second set – space‑filled improvisations, a blistering Scarlet > Fire transition, and a memorable Fire on the Mountain – while the first set remains solid despite a few lukewarm moments like Let It Grow. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the stronger late‑80s shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Four detailed reviews (three highly positive, one strongly negative) point to a high‑quality audience recording and an outstanding musical performance; the weighted average of star ratings and qualitative comments yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑board feel, well‑balanced and clear; occasional minor tape quirks noted but overall crisp and immersive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with relaxed openings; second set erupts into extended jams, strong transitions (Scarlet > Fire, So What), and a high‑energy finish that many reviewers cite as iconic for the era.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Teases through Scarlet, wails on Fire, scrubs strings on Fire on the Mountain; some criticism of his middle section in Estimated.
band_member_comments.Bob: New snarling vocal style noted positively; guitar tone on Sugar Magnolia praised as “record‑perfect”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Heavy tom work drives Scarlet > Fire and Space/So What; electronic/acoustic blend highlighted on Fire on the Mountain.
